title: quercus ilex var. grandifolia
intro: Quercus ilex var. grandifolia, also known as the Holm Oak or Evergreen Oak, is a subspecies of the Holm Oak tree, native to the Mediterranean region. It is a deciduous tree with medicinal properties, particularly in its bark, leaves, and acorns, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues. The medicinal properties of Quercus ilex var. grandifolia include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which can provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat conditions such as digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions.
